---
title: Paths
---

*Calepin* uses one rule for path resolution: **all input paths resolve relative to the document directory** (the parent of the `.qmd` file). There is no concept of "project root."

# Input paths

All path-bearing front matter fields -- `bibliography`, `csl`, `css`, and `{% include %}` directives -- resolve relative to the document directory. Markdown image paths and code chunk file I/O follow the same rule.

# Output paths

By default, output is written alongside the input file with the appropriate extension. The `-o` flag overrides this. Generated figures go in `_calepin_files/{stem}/` inside the output directory. The execution cache goes in `_calepin_cache/{stem}/` inside the document directory. Both are customizable via `calepin.files-dir` and `calepin.cache-dir` in front matter.

# Override resolution

Templates, element overrides, CSL files, and plugins follow a two-level fallback:

1. `_calepin/` in the document directory
2. Built-in defaults

Plugins resolve from `_calepin/plugins/{name}/plugin.yml`. Built-in plugins (tabset, layout, callout, theorem, figure-div, table-div) need no files on disk.

# Validation

*Calepin* validates `bibliography`, `csl`, and `calepin.plugins` paths before rendering. Missing files produce a hard error listing every unresolved path. Template and element overrides are not validated -- they silently fall back to built-in defaults.

# Summary

| Path | Resolves from | On missing |
|------|--------------|------------|
| `bibliography`, `csl`, `css` | Document directory | Hard error |
| `{% include %}` | Document directory | Error in output |
| Images, code chunk I/O | Document directory | -- |
| Plugins | `_calepin/plugins/` | Hard error |
| Templates, elements | `_calepin/` | Falls back to built-in |
| Output file | Document directory or `-o` | Created |
| `_calepin_files/` | Output directory | Created |
| `_calepin_cache/` | Document directory | Created |
